capital_adjusted: Late R1 / R2 top; Walk-On Flag +0.5 round upside; CRUSH premium confirmed; Tier A confidence
strengths: Elite pre-snap diagnostic speed and scheme processing; among the best ILB minds in the 2026 class. Walk-on story encapsulates the APEX character ideal: earned scholarship, earned starting role, earned first-round consideration through merit alone. Outstanding motor and effort consistency, elevates in high-leverage run fits and blitz packages.
red_flags: Athletic profile is below the elite tier for the position — lacks the closing speed and man-coverage athleticism to match up with slot receivers in dime packages. UCLA competition level (Pac-12/Big Ten transition) was not consistently SEC-quality, limiting SOS-adjusted production confidence. Projection as a two-down thumper without continued coverage development.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 1-10; Two-way premium; capital reflects top-side CB/WR position; Tier A confidence
strengths: Generationally unique two-way player with elite CB and WR production in the same season — a feat not accomplished at Power 5 level in decades. 9.5 athleticism profile combines top-end speed (sub-4.4 projection), elite jump ball skills, and exceptional change-of-direction. Two-Way Premium reflects genuine draft capital elevation: whichever side he plays, he profiles as a top-10 pick at that position.
red_flags: NFL teams must commit to one position; transition risk is real regardless of skill set. Two-way college workload means fewer reps in pure CB technique fundamentals (press technique, trail technique, jam footwork) compared to a single-position prospect. COMPRESSION gap reflects his elite balance across all archetypes rather than a concern — no dominant archetype because all are elite.

capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2 top) / R2; Tier C — ±1 round range, monitor; system dependency discount applied
strengths: Elite touch and accuracy from the pocket, NFL-ready release mechanics, and exceptional ability to navigate pressure and extend plays within his frame. Strong statistical output at Colorado against Power 4 competition demonstrates adaptability beyond his JSU baseline. Process as a passer, specifically his pre-snap identification and quick release reads, is genuinely high-floor.
red_flags: System dependency is the primary concern — Colorado's offense was architecturally designed around Sanders' skill set under Deion Sanders' bespoke scheme. Tier C confidence reflects legitimate unknowns: independent coaching relationship (Deion factor reduces confidence in c2/c3 baseline accuracy), limited scheme-diverse reps, and questions about translating production to an NFL system he did not design. RB athleticism profile limits scramble/extend threat value significantly. Capital carries ±1 round Tier C range.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32 / R1 Top depending on team need; Tier A confidence
strengths: Young and physically imposing OT with elite athleticism for the position — long arms, exceptional anchor strength, and outstanding short-area burst that stresses interior defenders. Missouri background provided meaningful SEC-adjacent competition, and Membou dominated in both run and pass protection. Developmental trajectory is among the best in this class: already dominant at a young age with clear ceiling remaining.
red_flags: COMPRESSION gap indicates he profiles well in multiple archetypes (athletic projection and complete prospect) without a dominant singular identity — a positive signal for this class. Still developing elite hand technique and combat consistency in pass rush, which will be stress-tested in the NFL. Limited power conference playoff depth at Missouri reduces SOS confidence.

capital_adjusted: Late R1 / R2 top; Tier A confidence
strengths: Elite run blocker with outstanding leverage, power, and contact-seeking mentality — every NFL run-first coordinator's ideal interior blocker. Four-year starter at Georgia with consistent dominance in SEC competition against NFL-caliber defensive linemen. Excellent competitive toughness and effort tail that elevates every snap.
red_flags: Athletic profile for an OG is solid but not exceptional; limited to run-heavy scheme alignments in full exploitation. Pass protection against elite speed rushers off the edge is his biggest projection concern — tendency to overextend hand placement under pressure. SOLID archetype gap is clean but not elite, reflecting a clear fit without the upside ceiling of the COMPRESSION tier.

capital_adjusted: R2 / R3 top; Tier A confidence
strengths: Well-rounded TE prospect with above-average athleticism and reliable hands in contested catch situations. Texas system provided meaningful opportunities against Big 12 pass rushers and coverage. Effective in both seam routes and in-line blocking, making him a viable two-way TE at the NFL level. TWEENER gap reflects balance between receiving-first and complete archetypes.
red_flags: Not an elite separator — relies on size and contested-catch ability rather than route precision or YAC creation. Blocking technique needs refinement at the point of attack against NFL-caliber defensive ends. His overall profile projects as a quality starter but not a top-5 TE in this class. Draft capital reflects good but not elite upside.

capital_adjusted: Day 3 / R4-R5 range; Tier B — ±0.5 round range; RB positional discount applied
strengths: Good short-area quickness and burst behind pads, effective in zone-read and inside zone concepts. Georgia production reflects good efficiency when healthy and playing behind an elite offensive line. Legitimate pass-catching ability from the backfield is a positive NFL-era trait.
red_flags: NO_FIT archetype gap reflects a profile that doesn't dominate any specific RB archetype — not elite enough to anchor as a feature back, not specialized enough to be a true satellite/receiving back, not physical enough as a between-the-tackles specialist. PVC of 0.70 (lowest positional premium) reflects market reality. Tier B confidence due to transfer context and inconsistent production volume. DEVELOPMENTAL tier is appropriate given the current trait-to-production ceiling alignment; projection requires scheme-specific development.

capital_adjusted: Late R1 / R2 top; SOS Gate note — production confidence Tier B; ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite athletic profile for the safety position — outstanding speed, range, and zone coverage ability that projects to premium box safety or single-high roles. South Carolina production showed consistent effort and playmaking against SEC competition. Physical profile and processing speed make him a legitimate early-round safety in the post-Ed Reed era of the position.
red_flags: SOS Gate applied: South Carolina schedule strength in 2025 did not include consistent elite competition, creating uncertainty in the production-quality floor. v_production discounted from raw assessment by -1.5 to reflect this. Tier B confidence due to SOS limitations. COMPRESSION gap reflects a complete safety profile without a singular dominant trait — athleticism leads but processing and versatility are nearly equal; this is a positive signal, not a concern.

capital_adjusted: R1 (late) / R2 top; Tier A confidence; development premium flagged
strengths: Elite athleticism and pass rush burst off the edge — combination of long arms, get-off speed, and bend around the corner that projects as a legitimate starter in any scheme. Boston College production was consistently high against ACC competition with multiple double-digit sack seasons. Developmental trajectory is strong; still adding counter moves to complement elite first-step.
red_flags: TWEENER gap reflects legitimate split between athletic projection and production machine archetypes — not a negative at this composite score, but signals he has not yet become the complete pass rusher his athleticism demands. BC competition level (ACC) is a step below SEC/Big Ten elite, limiting SOS confidence at the margins. Run stop and coverage deployment in complex NFL schemes requires developmental investment.

capital_adjusted: Late R1 / R2; Tier A confidence; production-led profile
strengths: Elite production from the interior at Ohio State against Big Ten competition — consistently penetrated against NFL offensive linemen and generated wins both against the run and in pass rush. Exceptional competitive toughness; does not take snaps off, high motor in pursuit. Athleticism profile for an IDL is above average with a quick first step and leverage advantage.
red_flags: SOLID archetype gap (not COMPRESSION) reflects a player who is clearly a Production Machine without elite versatility or developmental upside ceiling. IDL PVC of 0.90 reflects positional market reality. Route to elite production in the NFL requires continued counter-move development — currently relies heavily on power and leverage rather than diverse pass rush plan.

capital_adjusted: R2 / R3 top; Tier A confidence; C positional discount reflected
strengths: Elite center intelligence — outstanding pre-snap communication, protection call identification, and line calls make him a natural for NFL green-dot center duties. Pittsburgh background developed a complete center skill set across both run and pass protection. Processing speed at the center position is among the best in the 2026 class; veteran coaches consistently praise his film study.
red_flags: Athletic profile is serviceable but below elite for the position — lacks the burst to pull effectively in stretch schemes or reach block against explosive 3-techs. C PVC of 0.80 reflects positional market reality; centers rarely command premium capital regardless of quality. Production at Pitt (ACC competition) did not consistently stress-test against Big Ten or SEC interior talent.

capital_adjusted: R2 / R3; Tier A confidence; C positional discount; production-led profile
strengths: Dominant run blocker at Georgia against elite SEC competition — powered the Bulldogs' run game with consistent positional dominance at the point of attack. Outstanding physical profile for the center position with excellent anchor strength and hand power. SEC championship experience provides elite competition floor confidence.
red_flags: Processing and protection call intelligence is good but below elite — projects more as a run-first center than a pure green-dot communicator, which limits upside in pass-heavy schemes. C PVC of 0.80 reflects market reality. Development in pass protection technique (hand replacement, set mechanics under speed) is the key NFL translation variable.

capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2) - Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite athletic testing with 9.84 RAS showcases rare speed and explosion for the position. Demonstrates excellent versatility in coverage and as a pass rusher, creating mismatches in multiple alignments. High football IQ with strong pre-snap recognition and ability to adapt to different scheme deployments.
red_flags: Production doesn't fully match elite athletic profile, suggesting some development needed in translating physical tools to consistent on-field impact. Limited sample size as primary starter raises questions about sustained performance against top competition. Needs refinement in run-fitting fundamentals despite strong athletic foundation.

capital_adjusted: R1 Day 2 (R2) — Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite pre-snap recognition and diagnostic ability with exceptional command of defensive adjustments and protection calls. Strong competitive toughness and leadership qualities that translate to consistent game-to-game performance in the SEC. Solid character foundation with good work ethic and coachability that projects well to NFL development.
red_flags: Limited athletic ceiling with below-average speed and change of direction that could be exposed against elite NFL skill position players. Tier B confidence due to limited high-volume film sample and pre-combine measurables unknown. Development trajectory suggests steady but not explosive improvement curve.

capital_adjusted: R1 Top (early) - Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite athleticism with 9.87 RAS creates exceptional versatility as a hybrid linebacker who can cover, blitz, and range sideline-to-sideline. Strong processing ability allows him to diagnose plays quickly and adjust coverage responsibilities mid-snap. Physical tools translate to consistent tackling and the ability to match up with tight ends and running backs in coverage.
red_flags: Production numbers don't fully match the elite athletic profile, suggesting some inconsistency in application or scheme utilization. Limited high-level competition experience at Arizona State raises questions about translation to NFL-level speed and complexity. May be caught between positions without a clear NFL home if he can't settle into one primary role.

capital_adjusted: R1 top-10; EDGE premium; ELITE composite; Tier A confidence
strengths: Elite first-step quickness and bend around the arc; projects as an immediate Day 1 starter at a premium position. Pass-rush win rate against Power 4 tackles is elite. Motor is exceptional — plays through whistle and chases the ball consistently. Athleticism profile is top-tier for the position.
red_flags: Power-to-counter game requires NFL refinement — bend-heavy rushers occasionally struggle when tackles take away the arc. Production at Miami (FL) carries some recency bias from elevated team winning environment. Injury history must be clean-confirmed before top-10 capital.

capital_adjusted: R2 top; RB PVC suppression applied; raw quality elite but positional capital reality; Tier A confidence
strengths: Arguably the best RB in the 2026 class by raw profile — elite vision, burst, and contact balance. Receiving ability out of the backfield is well above average, projecting as a three-down back. Notre Dame production in premium competition gives strong SOS-adjusted floor. Elite acceleration through the hole.
red_flags: RB PVC (0.70) reflects structural positional value suppression — raw quality is elite but capital investment at RB is historically volatile. Durability at the position is a known concern for any RB entering early NFL workload. Teams may target him lower than consensus due to positional value.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32
strengths: Elite athletic profile with 9.99 RAS creates matchup advantages in coverage and as a versatile chess piece. Strong processing ability allows him to excel in multiple alignments and handle complex defensive assignments. Outstanding scheme versatility makes him valuable across different defensive systems.
red_flags: Production metrics suggest he hasn't fully maximized his elite athletic tools yet, indicating room for growth but also questions about consistency. Character profile shows solid work ethic but leadership qualities still developing at the college level.

capital_adjusted: R3 Tier B — ±0.5 round range
strengths: Physical run defender who consistently fills gaps and makes tackles at the point of attack. Shows good pursuit angles and plays with high motor throughout games. Demonstrates reliable tackling technique and can shed blocks effectively in confined spaces.
red_flags: Limited athletic testing raises concerns about coverage ability against faster skill position players. Processing speed appears adequate but not elite for pre-snap diagnosis. May struggle in space against modern passing offenses that stress linebackers in coverage.

capital_adjusted: R2 Tier B — ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite run-stopping instincts with exceptional gap discipline and physicality at the point of attack. Shows strong competitive toughness and consistent effort against Power 5 competition. Natural tackler with good production metrics and clean injury history.
red_flags: Processing speed can be inconsistent against complex passing concepts and RPO schemes. Limited athletic ceiling may restrict coverage versatility in modern NFL systems. Lacks elite pre-snap communication skills expected for green dot responsibilities.

capital_adjusted: R1 Top (early) - Tier B ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite gap-fitting ability with exceptional tackling consistency and sideline-to-sideline range. Physical presence at point of attack who consistently sheds blocks and makes tackles in traffic. Strong leadership qualities and high motor that translates to consistent effort across all four quarters.
red_flags: Limited in coverage against dynamic receiving threats and struggles with route anticipation in man coverage. Pre-combine athletic testing could reveal concerns about overall speed and change-of-direction ability. Some mechanical inconsistencies in coverage drops and pass rush technique need refinement at next level.

capital_adjusted: R2 - Tier B ±0.5 round range

red_flags: Processing speed against elite pass rushers remains inconsistent with some technique breakdowns under pressure. Limited exposure to top-tier edge rush talent raises questions about immediate readiness. Scheme versatility somewhat limited to zone-heavy concepts at this stage.

capital_adjusted: R2 early - positional value reality for pure runners limits ceiling despite elite traits

red_flags: Positional value ceiling limited by RB market dynamics and 0.70x PVC structural discount. Some concerns about long-term durability given physical running style. Limited sample size as primary back raises questions about sustained heavy usage at NFL level.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32, Tier B — ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite pass rush production with dominant hand technique and counter moves that translate across multiple fronts. Exceptional motor and competitive toughness shows up consistently in big games with reliable edge-setting against the run. Combines high-level processing with plus athleticism to win with both power and finesse moves.
red_flags: Limited elite competition exposure in Big 12 may inflate production metrics. Moderate injury concerns with previous shoulder issues that required management during senior season. Character profile shows solid work ethic but lacks the obsessive preparation habits of top-tier prospects.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32, Tier B — ±0.5 round range
strengths: Elite press technique and jam timing at the line of scrimmage, consistently disrupts receiver releases with physical coverage style. Advanced route recognition allows him to break on throws before receivers complete their breaks, generating consistent pass breakups in man coverage situations.
red_flags: Limited exposure to true elite receiver competition in SEC play, with most dominant performances coming against mid-tier receiving corps. Occasional lapses in zone coverage discipline when asked to play off-coverage, suggesting some scheme versatility concerns in pure zone systems.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32
strengths: Elite route precision and separation creation at all three levels, with exceptional hands and contested catch ability. Demonstrates advanced processing of coverage concepts and adjusts routes accordingly, showing NFL-ready route tree mastery. Consistent production against top-tier Big Ten competition with excellent ball skills in traffic.
red_flags: Speed testing will be critical as measurables remain unknown and could impact vertical separation ability at next level. Durability concerns after missing time with minor injuries, though nothing suggests long-term issues. Scheme versatility questioned as Ohio State's system may have provided favorable matchups that won't translate directly to NFL offensive concepts.

capital_adjusted: R1 Picks 11-32
strengths: Elite pre-snap processor who commands Utah's defense with exceptional anticipation and play diagnosis. Demonstrates advanced football IQ with ability to make line adjustments and communicate protection calls. Consistent high-level production against Pac-12 competition with excellent tackle efficiency and minimal missed assignments.
red_flags: Athletic limitations may restrict him in coverage against elite receiving threats and speed sweeps. Needs to prove he can maintain processing advantage against faster NFL game speed. May be scheme-dependent requiring defense to build around his cerebral strengths rather than raw athletic ability.

strengths: Elite technical anchor with exceptional pass protection fundamentals and hand placement. red_flags: Needs to continue developing raw power against elite interior pass rushers. Limited exposure to top-tier competition may require adjustment period at NFL level. Technique sometimes prioritized over physicality in run game situations.

strengths: Elite route precision and separation creation at all levels of the field with exceptional body control and timing. red_flags: Athletic limitations may cap ceiling against elite NFL cornerbacks, particularly in contested situations. Limited sample size against top-tier competition raises questions about projection. Needs to prove he can consistently separate against physical press coverage at the next level.
